items.question: Which method should you call on test data for a fitted CountVectorizer?
items.explanation: Test data must reuse vocabulary learned at fit time; transform applies the mapping without relearn. fit_transform would rebuild vocabulary from test strings.

items.question: What is the primary output shape convention of sklearn text vectorizers?
options: Sparse matrix: rows = documents, columns = terms
items.answer: Sparse matrix: rows = documents, columns = terms
items.explanation: Vectorizers emit scipy sparse matrices (often CSR) suitable for memory-efficient bag-of-words features.

items.question: Which vectorizer does NOT learn a vocabulary at fit time?
items.explanation: HashingVectorizer is stateless—it hashes tokens into fixed buckets without storing token-to-index maps.

items.question: Putting CountVectorizer and LogisticRegression in a Pipeline mainly prevents:
items.explanation: Pipeline ensures each CV fold fits the vectorizer on training folds only before transforming validation data.

items.question: Setting CountVectorizer(binary=True) changes output cells to:
items.explanation: binary=True caps any positive count at 1—presence features for Bernoulli-style models rather than frequency counts.
